title: Remote Desktop and Screen-Sharing Setup
text:
Q: How do I set up remote desktop and screen-sharing on my computer?
A: To set up remote desktop and screen-sharing, follow these steps for your operating system:

Windows:
1. Open the Start menu and type 'Remote Desktop Connection' in the search bar.
2. Click on 'Remote Desktop Connection' from the results.
3. In the Remote Desktop Connection window, enter the name or IP address of the computer you want to connect to.
4. Click 'Connect'.
5. Enter your credentials when prompted (username and password).
6. To enable screen sharing, ensure that the 'Allow remote connections to this computer' option is checked in the System Properties (accessible via Control Panel > System and Security > System > Remote settings).

macOS:
1. Open 'System Preferences' from the Apple menu.
2. Click on 'Sharing'.
3. Check the box next to 'Screen Sharing' to enable it.
4. Click 'Computer Settings' to set a password for VNC viewers if needed.
5. To connect to another Mac, open 'Finder', select 'Go' from the menu, and then 'Connect to Server'.
6. Enter the VNC address (vnc://[IP address]) of the computer you want to connect to and click 'Connect'.
7. Enter your credentials when prompted to access the remote screen.
